translated: I realized that my world and my expression can be as free as I want them to be,
and as Ena's world gradually gained color and her eyes sparkled with the joy of painting, I could really feel it through your voice.
It moved me to tears.
I want to keep watching over and supporting Ena as she resolves to never give up on her art and her dreams.
Minori-san, "the color of Ena's heart"... what a beautiful way to put it.
Thank you so much for bringing that to life so vividly through your performance.
Thank you for that message.
And thank you for taking those feelings to heart.
I always think about how I want Ena's heart to reach everyone.
Of course, she's a game character, a 2D existence,
but Ena is truly alive, and well...
No one knows what the future holds for her yet.
So, how should I put it?
I really want to deliver something that feels raw and real.
I'm so happy to hear your thoughts. Thank you.
All right.
Moving on...
There's so much I want to talk about while watching the story, so if I get too carried away now,
well,
five minutes will pass in a flash.
So let's keep things moving.
I've picked out some scenes that I personally want to talk about,
so let's watch them together.
I was a bit selfish with my choices today.
The staff are shaking their heads like "no, you weren't," but...
well,
I really struggled to choose.
I felt like I could just watch from Episode 1 to 8 with you all,
but
we're going to look at Episodes 7 and 8.
This is an AfterTalk, so I'll be doing a lot of talking.
Since I'll be chatting away,
if you find me too noisy, please go back and watch the scenes in the game later.
Okay, let's get started.
Episodes 7 and 8.
Here we go.
She's... calmed down.
I think she knew deep down all along.
Wait, was her account hacked?
Oh, it's gone.
This is Ena after making so many resolutions and steeling herself.
What does this mean?
That account for her selfies...
Those selfies were a bit of an escape for her,
something she relied on.
But she couldn't run away anymore.
She even went to the Peach Garden.
But she still lacked the confidence to tell those around her or even fully commit herself.
In this story,
she was finally able to truly make up her mind.
That's why.
It's painful from here on, but there's a sense of relief, like the fog in her heart has cleared.
I put all those complex feelings into it,
and I acted the final scenes while thinking about how Ena must feel relieved too.
The reason I chose Episodes 7 and 8 is because of her father. They've been through a lot and there were times she couldn't face him, but she really does admire him.

Her father isn't great with words, and Ena had hidden her respect for him behind her own frustration. But as she grew as an artist, she remembered those feelings. I also got to voice young Ena, who was so pure and saw the world as something sparkling and new, with her father right there in the middle of it.
To her, her father was someone she looked up to,
someone who could create incredible things.
He was like a mass of light.
I acted while keeping that image of talking to someone like that in mind, feeling the greatness of the art he creates.
Because I performed with that sparkle in my heart,
there were many scenes where she's told she can keep painting.
I performed those scenes without many breaks, just letting the emotions flow.
The scenery she saw with her father, the voice of her father...
It wasn't just about being held; it was about Ena finally being able to enjoy painting. Seeing her way of life reach everyone meant a lot to me personally.
I'm so happy that people are pleased and feel like they've been given a push forward.
Some said it made them want to do their best too.
For those who have been supporting Ena all this time, she's finally reached a point on her journey.
I'm so glad she finally made it here. But it's only just beginning.
Still, there were so many emotions...
I'm so moved by Ena.
It makes me so happy that everyone could empathize with her feelings.
Truly.
Actually, when I first saw the script, I burst into tears.
While reading it.
I'm so glad I could give it my all in the performance.
I really am.
I'm just so glad it's out there now.
That's how I feel.
